questions.questionText: Two forces act on a stationary object. A 12 N force acts to the right and an 8 N force acts to the left. What is the resultant force?

commonErrors: Adding the forces instead of subtracting
Stating the wrong direction for the resultant

questions.questionText: A trolley of mass 2.5 kg is pushed along a frictionless surface with a resultant force of 7.5 N. Calculate the acceleration of the trolley.

commonErrors: Multiplying F × m instead of dividing
Forgetting to include the unit m/s²

questions.questionText: An astronaut has a mass of 75 kg. The gravitational field strength on the Moon is 1.6 N/kg. Calculate the weight of the astronaut on the Moon.

commonErrors: Using g = 10 N/kg (Earth) instead of 1.6 N/kg (Moon)

questions.questionText: A 0.15 kg ball travelling at 20 m/s hits a wall and bounces back at 15 m/s. Calculate the change in momentum of the ball.

steps.description: Recognises direction change: momentum after = 0.15 × (-15) = -2.25 kg m/s
Correct unit: kg m/s and negative sign indicating direction change

allowedAlternatives: 5.25 kg m/s in the opposite direction
commonErrors: Forgetting that the ball changes direction so velocity is negative
Not using the correct formula for change in momentum

questions.questionText: A student pushes a box 4.0 m across a floor using a force of 35 N. Calculate the work done by the student.

commonErrors: Forgetting units or writing N instead of J

questions.questionText: A car is travelling at a constant speed along a straight road. Explain, in terms of forces, why the car travels at a constant speed.

markScheme.answer: The driving force equals the friction/air resistance, so the resultant force is zero and the car does not accelerate.

steps.description: The driving force is equal to the resistive forces (friction/air resistance)
By Newton's first law, an object with zero resultant force continues at constant velocity

commonErrors: Saying there are no forces acting on the car
Confusing constant speed with no forces rather than balanced forces
